structure.format: MCQ in sets of 2-5 questions tied to a primary or secondary source (text, image, map, chart, cartoon)
Short-Answer Questions, each with parts A, B, C. Answer Q1 + Q2 mandatory; choose Q3 OR Q4. Q1 typically stimulus-based, Q2 secondary-source-based, Q3/Q4 no stimulus. ~13 min per SAQ.
structure.rubric_constraints: Complete sentences required; outlines/bulleted lists not acceptable

structure.format: Document-Based Question with 7 documents. 15 min reading + 45 min writing.
Long Essay Question. Choose 1 of 3 options, each focused on a different time-period window. Suggested 40 min writing.

exam.scoring_scale: 1-5 with 3 passing (used by colleges for credit)

historical_reasoning_skills: Sourcing — Point of View, Purpose, Historical Situation, Audience
Argument Development (defensible thesis with line of reasoning)
Evidence Use (specific historical evidence beyond documents)
saq_format_template: A. Briefly describe one X. B. Briefly explain one Y. C. Briefly explain how Z. (each part = 1 point, complete sentences required)
vocabulary_cues: Briefly describe one major difference between X's and Y's historical interpretations
Briefly explain how one event or development from [period] not directly mentioned in the excerpts could be used to support X's argument
Briefly explain one development from [period] that contributed to
Evaluate the extent to which [topic] changed from [year] to [year]
Evaluate how [group/movement/policy] responded to [event] from [year] to [year]
Which of the following best describes the historical situation in which the passage was written?
Which of the following developments in the period [year]-[year] most directly led to the political situation referred to in the excerpt?
sample_stems_frq: SAQ stimulus pair: Historian Wilentz (2005) vs historian Bouton (2007) on early US democracy 1789-1820 — A. describe one difference, B. support Wilentz, C. support Bouton
SAQ primary source: Daniel Webster Senate speech 1830 — A. purpose of promoting union ideas, B. one 1820-1848 development, C. comparable 1848-1865 debate

DBQ: Evaluate the extent to which the role of the federal government in the United States economy changed from 1932 to 1980. (7 documents — WPA letter, War Food Admin pamphlet, Interstate Highway photo, JFK State of Union, Goldwater speech, César Chávez statement, Marjorie Holt 1976)
LEQ option: Evaluate how Native American societies adapted to the presence of European colonists in North America from 1500 to 1754
LEQ option: Evaluate how different reform movements in the United States responded to industrialization from 1820 to 1900
LEQ option: Evaluate how United States foreign policy responded to changes in the world from 1890 to 1930
